title: Bridge Essay: Habits without values.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Habit — automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, loss of conscious control.

**Habits without values.**. Psychol Rev.. PMID: 30676040. DOI: 10.1037/rev0000120. Habits form acrucial component of behavior. In recent years, key computational models have conceptualized habits as arising from model-free reinforcement learning mechanisms, which typically select between available actions based on the future value expected to result from each. Traditionally, however, habits have been understood as behaviors that can be triggered directly by astimulus, without requiring the animal to evaluate expected outcomes.
